# Sprint Spec Template

Use this template for sprint-level normative specs.

Rules:

- Write only verifiable criteria.
- Do not use vague phrases like "melhorar", "preparar para o futuro", "when applicable", "estrutura basica" or "deixar pronto".
- If a field is unknown, mark it explicitly as TBD and explain why.
- Do not let the spec contradict the Document Master, roadmap, backlog, handoff, or prompt.

## 1. Identification

- Spec ID:
- Sprint number:
- Spec title:
- Status:
- Date:
- Owner:
- Linked ADR:

## 2. Epic

- Epic ID:
- Epic name:
- Epic objective:
- Epic status:

## 3. Sprint objective

- Objective:
- Success condition:

## 4. Context

- Problem being solved:
- Why this sprint exists now:
- Historical context:

## 5. Dependencies

- Upstream documents:
- Product dependencies:
- Technical dependencies:
- Operational dependencies:

## 6. Histories included

- History ID:
- Type:
- Description:
- Acceptance criteria reference:

## 7. Histories not included

- History ID or description:
- Reason for exclusion:

## 8. Acceptance criteria by history

For each history, include:

- History ID:
- Criterion 1:
- Criterion 2:
- Criterion 3:
- Evidence expected:

## 9. Operational flow

- Entry condition:
- Main path:
- Exit condition:
- Failure path:

## 10. Contracts affected

- TypeScript types:
- API endpoints:
- Enums / statuses:
- UI contracts:

## 11. Frontend

- Routes affected:
- Screens affected:
- Behavior expected:
- Empty / loading / error states:

## 12. Backend

- Services or modules affected:
- Persistence behavior:
- Validation behavior:
- Auditing behavior:

## 13. Persistence

- Data model affected:
- Storage behavior:
- Restart behavior:
- Idempotency behavior:

## 14. Audit

- Events to record:
- Required metadata:
- Redaction rules:

## 15. Costs

- Cost events:
- Cost limits:
- Cost evidence:

## 16. Security and compliance

- Security constraints:
- Compliance gates:
- Secret handling:
- Access restrictions:

## 17. Out of scope

- Explicit exclusions:
- Future work not included:

## 18. Probable files

- Files expected to change:
- Files that should not change:

## 19. Test strategy

- Unit tests:
- Integration tests:
- UI tests:
- Regression tests:
- Validation commands:

## 20. Evidence

- Required screenshots:
- Required logs:
- Required reports:
- Required links:

## 21. Risks

- Risk:
- Why it matters:
- Mitigation:

## 22. Definition of Ready

- Story linkage:
- Description:
- Acceptance criteria:
- Dependencies:
- Affected contracts:
- Expected evidence:
- Scope compatibility:

## 23. Definition of Done

- Implementation complete:
- Criteria met:
- Tests passed:
- Documentation updated:
- Security reviewed:
- Audit reviewed:
- Cost reviewed:
- Evidence available:
- Pending items recorded:

## 24. Gate of the sprint

- Gate name:
- Objective condition:
- Verification method:
- Pass criteria:
- Block criteria:
